cards.title: Emotional Maturity and Raising Happy, Healthy Children
cards.body: Psychologist Lindsay C Gibson's book 'Adult Children of Emotionally Immature Parents' became an unexpected pandemic bestseller. She has since written new advice focused on raising happy, healthy children. The book addresses how parents can achieve emotional maturity to better support their children's development.

cards.title: Parenting Teenagers Taking A-Levels: Navigating Exam Season
cards.body: Parents of teenagers studying A-levels often feel powerless and uncertain when helping with subjects like chemistry or further maths. The experience of holding flashcards and supporting exam preparation can be overwhelming for parents unfamiliar with advanced coursework. This explores the unique challenges parents face during their teenager's crucial exam years.

cards.title: What to Do When You Don't Like Your Teenager
cards.body: Parenting teenagers during their sometimes difficult years requires specific strategies to ease family tensions. Experts provide guidance on handling your child's attitude and behavior challenges. The article offers practical advice for parents struggling with their relationship during the teenage years.

cards.title: Minimizing Negative Effects of Screen Time on Sleep
cards.body: Research shows adolescents' use of smartphones and other devices significantly impacts sleep quality. Recent studies have examined how screen time affects teenage sleep patterns and overall health. The article summarizes findings on managing device use to protect children's sleep.

cards.title: Body Play and Child Development: What Parents Need Know
cards.body: Parents should be prepared if they find children playing together without clothes, as this is developmentally normal behavior. Understanding age-appropriate exploration helps parents respond calmly and educationally. The article provides guidance on handling these situations as part of healthy childhood development.

cards.title: BFRB Treatment: Supporting Children Not Yet Ready to Stop
cards.body: Body-focused repetitive behaviors like hair pulling and skin picking often require treatment even when children aren't ready to stop the behavior. Meaningful BFRB work can begin before willingness arrives through supportive therapeutic approaches. The article explains how parents can help their children manage these challenging behaviors.

cards.title: What Fathers Truly Want: Beyond Traditional Father's Day Gifts
cards.body: Research shows many fathers value feeling loved, appreciated, and truly seen by their families more than material gifts. Father's Day gifts do not need to be complicated or expensive to be meaningful. The article highlights what children can do to make their fathers feel genuinely valued.

cards.title: Coping with Emotional Toll of Infertility for Families
cards.body: Infertility brings grief, uncertainty, anxiety, and isolation that others may not fully understand, affecting family planning and mental health. Parents and prospective parents navigating infertility face unique emotional challenges. The article addresses the psychological impact and support needed for families experiencing fertility issues.

cards.title: Social Connection Initiative: Tables to Talk and Community
cards.body: A new mother in Manchester, England, struggling with limited social interaction found a solution that has now spread throughout the UK. The initiative creates spaces for parents and caregivers to connect and build community. This demonstrates how simple interventions can address parental isolation and support family wellbeing.
